titleStmt.title: SII 25.159: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 159. (A.R. No. 159 of 1908.) TIRUNAṞAIYŪR, KUMBAKONAM TALUK, THANJAVUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: South wall of the central shrine in the Siddhanāthasvāmi temple.

This built in inscription refers to the king Uttamachōḷa, as being born to Pirāntakaṉ Mādēvaḍigaḷār alias Sembiyaṉ-Mādēviyār and registers the grant of 6 vēli of lands for the food-offerings daily as well as on Saṅkrānti days and for the sacred cloths (pariśaṭṭam) to the deity Siddhēśvaramuḍaiya-mahādēva at Tirunaṟaiyūr-nāḍu. Also it records the grant of a taxfree dēvadāṉa land in the village Mēlaḍavāy in the same nāḍu. It is stipulated that the brāhmaṇa who performs worship in the above temple should receive ten nāḻi of paddy, probably for his maintenance. The place is referred to in the hymns of Tēvāram.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrī Uttama coḻa devarait tiruvayiṟuvāytta Uṭaiyapirāṭṭiyār śrī parāntakaṉ mātevaṭikaḷāṉa śrīcempiyaṉ māteviyār tirunaṟaiyūr nā teyam tirunaṟaiyūr sitdheśvaramuṭaiya mahādevarkku tiruvamutukkum m
-t nilam 6veli Itaṉāl ceyyumaṟṟa nivantaṅkaḷ muṉṟu potiṉukku tiruvamutarici potiṉukkum kaṟiyamutinukkum ḷa muṉṟu poḻutukkum neyyamutu muṉṟu potiṉukku Aṭaikkāy
ḻukkai ciṟāva muṉṟu potum Amutu ceyya vāḻaippaḻam 10 2(1/2)ḷa �m muṉṟuThe rest of the record is damaged.
Digital edition of SII 25.159 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
